definitions: A structure containing a number of nerve cell bodies, typically linked by synapses, and often forming a swelling on a nerve fiber.
A cystic swelling that occurs on a joint capsule or tendon sheath, typically on the back of the wrist.
examples: The surgeon removed the ganglion from her wrist.
Nerve impulses are processed in the ganglion.
He developed a ganglion after the injury.
